entry.title: Washington Post: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Severodonetsk is ‘dead,’ Zelensky says; Moscow rebuked at U.N. for fueling food crisis
  • Lilibet turns 1, and photos show Harry and Meghan’s daughter loving it
  • China’s university entrance exams are already stressful. Now add covid.
  • The Trump-like TikTok star who could be Colombia’s next president
  • ‘Needle spiking’ fears rise in Europe, but crime ‘really difficult’ to trace
  • Millions looted from Afghan government during Taliban takeover
  • Kyiv asks for more rocket systems as Kremlin warns of potential strikes in Russia
  • Fighting in Severodonetsk has ‘worsened,’ official says; Britain pledges rocket systems
  • India scrambles to contain fallout over insulting comments about Islam
  • The world’s largest four-day workweek pilot just launched in the U.K.
  • The diplomatic war over Ukrainian grain exports heats up
  • Ukraine’s position has ‘worsened’ in fight for Severodonetsk
  • Cholera fears prompt quarantine in Mariupol, official says
  • Boris Johnson survives but is weakened by no-confidence vote
  • Who would replace Boris Johnson? Here are some of the contenders.
  • How Boris Johnson went from landslide victory to no-confidence vote
  • Milley equates ‘horrors’ in Ukraine with suffering during World War II
  • Church attack rattles area of Nigeria where religious violence is rare
  • China secretly building PLA naval facility in Cambodia, Western officials say
  • German prosecutors probe 3 rail workers over fatal crash
  • Trial resumes of suspects in slaying of Dutch crime reporter
  • Church convicts Catholic ex-priest of abusing boy for years
  • Dubai arrests 2 Gupta brothers over South African fraud case
  • Toru Takahashi, respected AP Asia photo editor, dies at 62
  • Indonesian army officer gets life sentence for double murder
  • Man drowns as Arizona police watch: ‘I’m not jumping in after you’
  • She found $36,000 inside a chair she got for free on Craigslist
  • Families sue Uvalde gunman, signal possible action against gunmaker
  • After shooting, Uvalde residents say need for mental health care is high
  • Anger and heartbreak on Bus No. 15
  • Primary elections live updates: Voters in California, six other states to decide primaries
  • Mexico’s president snubs Biden’s invitation to summit
  • Proud Boys leader Tarrio, 4 lieutenants charged with seditious conspiracy
  • Post Politics Now: Jan. 6, guns on the radar for Congress; Biden announces latest baby formula flight
  • Crypto industry scores a big win under long anticipated Senate bill
  • White House takes steps to spur solar industry
  • How to screen remote-learning apps for privacy
  • How to bring stamped concrete back to life
  • Contemporary, 300-unit apartment complex opens in Alexandria
  • Common lease terms renters should know before signing on dotted line
  • New York City Is a Lot Safer Than Small Town America
  • Israel Makes the Right Choice on Natural Gas Exploration
  • Should the US Have a Monarchy?
  • CEOs Love to Hear ‘Yes’ But Need ‘No, No, No’
  • How a Battery Metals Squeeze Puts EV Future at Risk
  • ‘Fake’ Aluminum Stocks Put Perils of China’s Commodities Funding in Spotlight
  • What’s Behind Vietnam’s Latest Anti-Corruption Fight
  • What the Jan. 6 Committee Has Done, and What’s Next
  • What inflation struggles of the past can tell the Fed right now
  • Why Black people are afraid of ‘crazy’ White people
  • The Jan. 6 committee’s audience won’t match Watergate’s. But it should.
  • We already achieved ‘energy independence.’ What good did it do us?
  • Eric Holder on the violent past and imperiled future of voting
  • This Pride Month, I’m embracing ‘rainbow capitalism’
  • Most Americans don’t accept the mass slaughter of children. Why does the GOP?
  • No school should have to close because of extreme heat
  • Free speech can’t trump every other value on campus
  • Vladimir Kara-Murza from jail: Don’t forget Russia’s political prisoners
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: Guardian: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Boris Johnson’s no-confidence vote: PM tells cabinet to ‘draw a line’ under Partygate after narrowly surviving bruising ballot – live

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 7 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-07 10:31AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong snack chain Aji Ichiban shuts all 20 retail outlets amid losses from lack of tourists during pandemic
  • Life in Hong Kong’s shoebox housing - complete visual explainer
  • Heavy rain exposes dummy grenade sparking bomb scare at Hong Kong army barracks
  • Hong Kong tram fares to rise to HK$3, a steeper increase than expected, as operator struggles with fall in ridership and shrinking revenue
  • Hong Kong’s self-styled ‘Captain America’ spouted ‘empty words’, legal counsel says in first appeal related to city’s national security law
  • Tickets for Hong Kong Palace Museum to cost between HK$50 and HK$120, with venue to open on July 2
  • Out-of-control truck pins police car against barrier, crashes into 2 vehicles during Hong Kong morning rush hour
  • Nan Hai says creditors are freezing its assets as it comes up short to meet US$350 million of notes due this weekend
  • Hong Kong extends regulatory rules to NFT-based collective investment schemes, warning investors to be mindful of risks
  • Hong Kong leader Carrie Lam defends police action to close off Victoria Park on June 4, saying anniversary of Tiananmen Square crackdown could spur violent activism
  • ESF kindergartens provide the perfect environment for young learners to flourish and grow
  • Coronavirus: too early to suggest arrival of sixth wave, Hong Kong leader Carrie Lam says; city reports 489 new cases
  • Hong Kong counterterrorism unit set to launch hotline for tip-offs on suspicious activities with cash rewards for useful information
  • Hong Kong police to mobilise all officers for possible state leader visit at handover bash, force chief says
  • Hong Kong police chief warns viewers to avoid documentary about city’s social unrest over concerns of possible national security violations
  • Funding for Hong Kong child protection group cut by HK$1.8 million after abuse allegations
  • Hong Kong’s top officials could be put under week-long ‘closed-loop’ arrangement ahead of 25th handover anniversary celebrations
  • Former Hong Kong Bar Association chairman tipped to become justice minister in John Lee’s governing team, with incumbent Teresa Cheng on the way out
  • Can Joe Biden’s economic package overcome Americas summit exclusion row?
  • China’s 30/60 goals: Beijing unveils ‘transition bonds’ for eight dirty industries, to meet carbon reduction targets
  • Gruelling gaokao exams put China’s ‘epidemic generation’ to the test
  • China plans to start building first-ever solar power plant in space by 2028
  • China plans sweeping makeover of academic journals to raise the profile and influence of domestic scientific research
  • Kuomintang chairman, in Washington, pitches his party as stabilising presence between Beijing and Taipei
  • China’s foreign minister in Kazakhstan as Ukraine, Afghanistan uncertainties rumble
  • China’s unemployment woes see Yunnan offer big cash subsidies to entice college graduates
  • Chinese scientists say ‘Midas’ touch helps to fine-tune CRISPR gene editing
  • EU lawmakers push to designate human rights conditions in Xinjiang as ‘genocide’
  • US suspends tariffs on some solar panel imports for two years, but leaves China out
  • How the economic slowdown and zero-Covid threaten China’s global ambitions
  • China accuses Canada of stepping up reconnaissance against Chinese military
  • Coronavirus: Chinese tourists trickle back during Dragon Boat Festival, but far short of pre-pandemic levels
  • Shanghai reopening: Tesla’s Chinese buyers face up to 24 weeks for delivery as carmaker scrambles to catch up with demand in ‘China’s Motown’
  • Thailand urged to let refugee status of stranded Rohingya be evaluated by UN
  • What caused a Malaysian man to slice his mother into 15 pieces in alleged murder?
  • US, S Korea fly 20 fighter jets over N Korea in ‘swift and forceful’ response, a day after warning
  • Oscar-winning Japanese musician ‘The Last Emperor’ Sakamoto battling stage IV cancer
  • US warns of ‘forceful response’ to anticipated North Korea nuclear test
  • Tourists to Japan may be sent home if they don’t wear face masks and follow other health measures, government says
  • India under fire as Muslim nations lash out over Prophet Mohammed remarks
  • Australia’s lettuce shortage forces KFC to put cabbage in its burgers
  • Singapore airfares cost 27 per cent more than before Covid-19 – so why are prices so high?
  • China ‘testing’ Tokyo with underwater survey off Japan’s coast, analysts say
  • Sri Lanka’s Rajapaksa to finish term despite protests: ‘I can’t go as failed president’
  • Indian products pulled by Kuwait supermarket in row over Prophet remarks
  • Donald Trump reposts edited image of his likeness on Mount Rushmore, next to George Washington
  • Ukraine war: Russia bans 61 more Americans as US media face visa, accreditation denial
  • Blinken says reports of Russia stealing Ukraine grain for profit ‘credible’
  • Ukraine’s Zelensky says Russian troops intend to capture Zaporizhzhia, after fall of Kherson and Mariupol
  • ‘Greased piglet’ Boris Johnson survives as UK prime minister. For now
  • UK reports 77 new monkeypox cases, biggest outbreak outside Africa
  • Shock as Iraq jails British man Jim Fitton for 15 years over smuggling artefacts
  • Gupta brothers accused of defrauding South Africa arrested in UAE
  • Two-year-old US boy shoots and kills father after finding gun at home
  • UK’s Boris Johnson survives no-confidence vote after partygate rebellion
  • Taser-maker Axon halts controversial project to equip drones with stun guns
  • Top Gun heirs sue Paramount over Maverick sequel
  • Ukraine war: US orders two Roman Abramovich jets seized over Russia sanctions
  • Mass shootings now define American exceptionalism
  • Ukraine recovers steel plant siege bodies from last-ditch stand in Mariupol
  • South Africans visiting UK on Ryanair quizzed in Afrikaans to prove identity
  • Investors brand Greenland’s bond payment extension unfair as credibility of state-backed companies comes into question
  • How 3 insurance professionals have helped Hong Kong tackle challenges such as Covid-19 pandemic
  • Citic Securities tops China’s brokerage industry charts, beating peers in assets, revenue and profit
  • LME in court again, faces US$15.3 million suit from trading firm Jane Street Global for cancelling nickel futures trades
  • Why Shanghai reopening is good for Biden and global economy
  • Hong Kong landlords and sellers should brace themselves for a more realistic property market in the near term
  • Traditional bricks-and-mortar toolmaker turns to digital commerce platform Vtex to help it on its digitalisation journey
  • Hong Kong stocks slip from two-month high as Covid-19 cases rebound while Chinese tech firms surrender gains
  • Tencent, Alibaba backed Chinese start-ups G7 Connect, E6 Technology merge to create truck management services giant
  • Hong Kong real estate owners seek alternative uses for retail complexes and hotels amid tourism slump
  • Climate change: UK-backed initiative aims to tackle greenwashing with globally recognised emissions standards
  • What Singapore can teach Canada about curbing home prices without the xenophobia
  • Chinese carmakers get V-sign from analysts as tax cuts seen reviving share prices, sales from April slump
  • Elon Musk says Twitter’s lack of information on bots breaches terms of their merger deal
  • China’s box office receipts hit 10-year low as ongoing Covid-19 restrictions produce a sad storyline for country’s cinemas
  • Amazon cloud’s latest collaboration with Hong Kong Science Park seen as vote of confidence in city’s role as regional tech hub
  • Why are morel mushrooms so loved by Hong Kong chefs? They’re bursting with savoury flavour and full of amino acids and antioxidants
  • Queer artist Oscar yi Hou blends Asian and American imagery to explore ideas of migration, identity and belonging
  • Lamborghini, Ferrari, Maserati, Ducati and Pagani luxury sports cars and bikes all come from the same part of Italy – a visit to Motor Valley
  • How endangered languages in countries like Indonesia and Malaysia are a vital tool in saving the marine environment
  • Pride Month 2022: how do you talk about LGBTQ issues? Better education would go a long way, says Hong Kong rights advocate
  • When Stephen Hawking visited Hong Kong in 2006, famed scientist had a rock-star welcome at the airport
  • Hong Kong has always had foreign residents who found the city incredibly boring
  • 10 summer health tips from Ayurveda experts to avoid excess body heat and stay healthy
  • 2022 LVMH Prize jury rewarded young fashion designers, and Cate Blanchett, Louis Vuitton’s Nicolas Ghesquière and Delphine Arnault were there with advice and support
  • Why depression in old age is different, and how to deal with it – in yourself or a loved one
  • Not just for royals: Queen Elizabeth and Meghan Markle don tiaras of course, but now so do Blake Lively, Madonna, Angelababy and other A-listers on the red carpet …
  • K-drama queens who don’t look a day over 25: Jang Na-ra, Jun Ji-hyun, Ha Ji-won, Go Hyun-jung and Han Go-eun share their anti-ageing beauty, fitness and diet tips – here’s how they do it
  • Inside Sourav Ganguly’s new US$5 million family home: the ‘Prince of Kolkata’ and Indian cricket legend splashed out on his first move in 48 years from his hometown of Behala
  • How royal titles will change when Prince Charles becomes king: Queen Elizabeth just celebrated her Platinum Jubilee, but what will William, Harry and Kate Middleton be called in future?
  • Who is the father of Amber Heard’s baby? Meet daughter Oonagh Paige: during the Johnny Depp case, the actress spoke out about being a mum, with plenty of fans wondering if Elon Musk is the baby daddy
  • How Madonna’s son David Banda is following in her footsteps: the 16 year old from Malawi made headlines by wearing an Adidas x Gucci dress as tribute to his famous mum – and he sings and dances too
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Government to ask public to save electricity this summer, winter
  • Suspect in COVID subsidy fraud fled as scheme began to unravel
  • Japan to open to tourists after two years but only with masks, insurance, guides
  • Universities in Japan continue trend of moving to urban areas
  • TEPCO issues 1st apology from president for nuclear accident
  • Earlier start to rainy season called for Kanto-Koshin region
  • Increased U.S. military flights into Okinawa anger residents
  • Oldest person to sail Pacific alone keeps his next adventure secret
  • Sapporo rejects holding referendum over bidding for Olympics

  • Tohoku’s only bullfighting event celebrates 40th season
  • Opposition attacks ‘Kishida inflation’ ahead of Diet election
  • Ministry studies war in Ukraine to prepare Japan for invasion
  • Kishida looks to be first Japanese leader to attend a NATO summit
  • Nippon Ishin aims to win Diet seat in Kyoto at CDP’s expense
  • LDP holds its 1st campaign speech meeting in the metaverse
  • Clean energy strategy to seek ‘maximum’ use of nuclear power
  • Eneos acquires 4,600 electric vehicle chargers from NEC Corp.
  • BOJ governor: Japanese now more tolerant of higher prices
  • Nobuyuki Idei, former chief executive of Sony Corp., dies at 84
  • Entrepreneur gets creative for benefit of firm and employees
  • China-Canada tensions rising again over North Korea air patrols
  • Beijing reopens restaurants as new COVID-19 cases drop
  • China says Canadian military jets increased reconnaissance, provocations
  • Seoul’s Blue House a tourist hot spot after opening to public
  • U.S., S. Korea fly 20 fighter jets amid North Korea tensions
  • U.S. official says will respond swiftly in case of North Korea nuclear test
  • North Korean building work advancing at nuclear site, IAEA says
  • U.S., South Korea fire missiles to sea, matching North’s launches
  • Sri Lanka holds its breath as new PM fights to save economy
  • Don’t call us pro-China, Taiwan opposition chief says in U.S.
  • Asian stocks rise as survey shows Chinese services reviving
  • Biden orders emergency steps to boost U.S. solar production
  • Fierce street fighting in Ukraine’s Sievierodonetsk, a pivotal battle for Donbas
  • Damaged, UK’s Boris Johnson scrapes win in party confidence vote
  • Australian leader’s visit affirms deeper ties to Indonesia
  • British Prime Minister Johnson to face confidence vote
  • Google told to pay Australian politician $515,000 over defamatory YouTube videos
  • Russia hits Kyiv with missiles; Putin warns West on arms
  • ‘Building blocks of life’ found in soil taken from asteroid Ryugu
  • 13-year-old go prodigy becomes youngest to tally 100 victories
  • Footprints of monsters, energy beam celebrate ‘Shin Ultraman’
  • Lisa’s Wanderings Around Japan/ Makino Botanical Garden: Preserving the legacy of Japan’s ‘father of botany’
  • EDITORIAL: Open discourse with public key to reform efforts for Nihon University
  • EDITORIAL: New regulations needed to rein in Apple/Google OS phone duopoly
  • VOX POPULI: Meteorite soil may show the ‘umami of life’ came from space
  • VOX POPULI: As weeds flourish in rainy season, a reflection on what they cover
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.content:
